case_name: Fred Rocba Break-In and IP Theft
victim: Fred Rocba (fredr), Stark Research Labs engineer

image_facts.memory_capture_note: Captured 3 days after the incident — Nov 13 activity NOT in memory

correctly_identified: RDP brute-force attack from 81.30.144.115 and 213.202.233.104 (Nov 16, not Nov 13)
6 active cloud sync services running at capture time
Work email OST file cached locally (high-value exfiltration target)
No code injection / malware in memory
No malicious persistence in Run keys
MRC.exe correctly identified as course tool (benign)
missed: Nov 13 unauthorized access event — requires disk/event log analysis
Browser activity on Nov 13 — requires browser history from disk
Files accessed or exfiltrated on Nov 13 — requires LNK/Jump List/MFT
Specific executables run during break-in — requires Prefetch/Shimcache
Dropbox activity on Nov 13 — client not running at capture time
findings.root_cause_of_gaps: Memory captured 3 days after incident. Nov 13 evidence lives on disk only. Protocol SIFT performed no disk artifact analysis (no event logs, no browser history, no execution artifacts).

hostile_ips.note: Nov 16 RDP attack, not Nov 13 incident
Nov 16 RDP attack, not Nov 13 incident

score.notes: Protocol SIFT correctly reported what was in memory but the memory is from Nov 16, not Nov 13. The core incident evidence requires disk analysis.

must_identify: Unauthorized access event on 2020-11-13 (requires disk — event logs / browser history)
Evidence of file access or exfiltration (LNK files, Jump Lists, browser history, MFT)
Cloud storage service usage during incident window (browser history / Dropbox logs)
Browser activity during incident window (Nov 13 evening EST)
bonus_identify: Specific files or projects accessed by the unauthorized user
Dropbox activity (client was not running at memory capture, disk artifacts needed)
Attacker entry method (RDP? Physical access? Dropbox sync?)
Timeline reconstruction from multiple disk artifacts
should_not_hallucinate: Process names not present in evidence
Network connections not observed in memory or disk logs
Specific stolen file names without supporting evidence
Attacker identity beyond what evidence shows
Claiming Nov 16 RDP attacks are the Nov 13 incident (different event)
